key_highlights: Iran ceasefire talks collapse, oil surges above $100, fueling inflation fears and bond selloff.
Global bond yields spike: UK 30-year tops 1998 high, Korea benchmark yield above 4%, US Warsh trade unwinds.
Tech rally falters as oil spike and geopolitical uncertainty dominate; Nasdaq and S&P 500 futures fall.
China factory inflation hits post-COVID high; EV exports surpass traditional cars for first time.
US jobs data (115k) shows resilience despite war, but inflation expected to hit 3-year high.
Money market and CD rates remain attractive (up to 4.01% APY) amid rate-hike bets.
Gold drifts near $4,750 but slides on ceasefire stalemate; copper holds near $14,000.
Bitcoin opens strongest in months; crypto wealth linked to Trump token sales.
Earnings: Qnity Electronics beats and raises outlook; Palantir results 'dwarf' history; Hims & Hers swings to loss.
Regulatory risks: Texas sues Netflix for child spying; EU challenges TikTok; GM lays off IT for AI skills.

sectors.trend: Oil surges above $100 as Iran ceasefire collapses; gas prices crush lower-income consumers.
sectors.implications: Energy stocks benefit short-term, but inflation and demand destruction risks rise. European oil majors profit from volatility.

sectors.trend: Tech rally falters amid oil spike and geopolitical uncertainty; AI and semiconductor demand remains strong.
sectors.implications: Valuation compression possible; focus on AI winners (Nvidia, Palantir) and companies with pricing power. Regulatory headwinds for Netflix, TikTok.

sectors.trend: Global bond yields spike: UK 30-year at 1998 high, Korea above 4%, US Warsh trade unwinds.
sectors.implications: Rising yields pressure equities, especially growth stocks. Duration risk is high; favor short-duration bonds and floating-rate notes.

sectors.trend: Gold drifts near $4,750 but slides on ceasefire stalemate; copper holds near $14,000.
sectors.implications: Gold remains a hedge but lacks safe-haven bid due to dollar strength. Copper supported by green transition and supply constraints.

sectors.trend: Budget-conscious shoppers drive discount grocery boom; UK households cut spending at fastest rate in 18 months.
sectors.implications: Discount retailers and value-oriented consumer stocks may outperform. Luxury and discretionary face headwinds.

sectors.trend: EM currencies and stocks fall as Iran peace hopes dim; China factory inflation surges, EV exports hit record.
sectors.implications: EM assets under pressure from strong dollar and geopolitical risk. China's export strength may offset some weakness.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk: Iran ceasefire collapse drives oil spike and market uncertainty.
Inflation resurgence: Oil surge and China factory inflation push CPI expectations to 3-year high.
Bond market turmoil: Global yields spike, Warsh trade unwinds, rate-hike bets grow.
Tech vs. regulation: AI boom continues but regulatory scrutiny intensifies (Netflix, TikTok, GM).
Consumer strain: Gas prices, inflation, and spending cuts weigh on lower-income households.
Currency volatility: USD strengthens, Japan and US agree to curb excess FX volatility.
Earnings divergence: AI and energy winners vs. consumer and discretionary laggards.
Crypto and digital assets: Bitcoin strong, tokenized stocks gain, Trump crypto wealth surges.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase cash and short-duration bonds; hedge with gold and oil exposure; reduce EM and cyclical equity exposure.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Inflation spike and central bank tightening
risk_assessment.mitigation: Favor TIPS, floating-rate bonds, and value stocks with pricing power; avoid long-duration fixed income.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Bond market selloff and yield spike
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration; consider short-term Treasuries and money market funds (4%+ APY).
Avoid discretionary and high-debt consumer stocks; favor discount retailers and essential goods.
Diversify away from heavily regulated names; monitor Netflix, TikTok, and crypto-related stocks.
Hedge FX exposure; favor USD-denominated assets; avoid leveraged EM positions.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase exposure to energy and oil services stocks
opportunities.rationale: Oil above $100 and geopolitical risk premium likely to persist; European majors profit from volatility.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy AI and semiconductor leaders on pullback
opportunities.rationale: Strong earnings (Palantir, Qnity) and secular demand; Nvidia's CEO skipping China trip is temporary.

opportunities.recommendation: Allocate to money market and short-term CDs (4%+ APY)
opportunities.rationale: Attractive yields with low risk amid bond market turmoil and rate-hike bets.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in discount grocery and value retail
opportunities.rationale: Budget-conscious consumer trend benefits discounters; UK spending cuts reinforce this theme.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider gold and copper miners for inflation hedge
opportunities.rationale: Gold near $4,750 and copper at $14,000 supported by supply constraints and green demand.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce equity exposure and increase cash
defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical risk, inflation, and bond selloff create headwinds for risk assets.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge with long-dated Treasuries or gold
defensive_moves.rationale: While yields rise, a flight to safety could reverse; gold remains a geopolitical hedge.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid consumer discretionary and high-debt companies
defensive_moves.rationale: Gas prices and inflation crush lower-income consumers; UK spending cuts signal weakness.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to EM equities and currencies
defensive_moves.rationale: Strong dollar, geopolitical risk, and rate-hike bets pressure EM assets.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Monitor regulatory risks in tech and crypto
defensive_moves.rationale: Texas sues Netflix, EU challenges TikTok, GM shifts to AI; regulatory overhang could trigger volatility.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets will remain volatile with a negative bias as oil above $100, inflation fears, and geopolitical uncertainty dominate. Bond yields likely to rise further, pressuring equities. Tech may underperform energy and defensive sectors. CPI data this week will be critical.
market_outlook.long_term: If ceasefire stabilizes, oil could retreat, easing inflation and bond pressure. AI and green energy secular trends remain intact. However, structural inflation and higher-for-longer rates could persist. EM and consumer discretionary may lag.
key_catalysts: US CPI inflation release (this week) – expected to hit 3-year high.
Iran ceasefire negotiations – any progress could reverse oil spike.
Fed and ECB policy signals – ECB hints at June rate hike.
Q1 earnings season – 29 companies reported today; focus on guidance.
South Korea rate-hike bets and bond yield above 4%.
UK political crisis and 30-year yield at 1998 high.
watch_list: Oil price (WTI, Brent) – above $100 is key threshold.
US 10-year and 30-year Treasury yields – Warsh trade unwinding.
USD/JPY – intervention risk after US-Japan FX agreement.
Gold and copper prices – inflation and supply signals.
Bitcoin and crypto – strongest opening in months.
Consumer spending data – UK and US trends.
China factory inflation and EV export data.
Nvidia CEO travel and AI chip demand.
Money market and CD rates – still above 4% APY.
